description: A deposit of small white ice crystals formed on surfaces when the temperature falls below freezing. 'Theriko' names winter's delicate art—the crystalline patterns that decorate windows and leaves, cold made visible in intricate beauty.
sound_symbolism: The word crystallizes in cold. 'The' /θe/ begins with cold breath, the chill air. Then 'ri' sharpens into crystal, the frozen pattern forming. Finally 'ko' covers surfaces, the frost spreading its lacework.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo theriko' (the frost). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Related to 'kerithe' (ice) as lighter frozen form. Associated with cold seasons, delicate patterns, and morning chill.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Frost creates nature's finest lacework—window frost especially inspired Art Nouveau with its organic, crystalline patterns.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Frost teaches that transformation can be beautiful—water becoming crystal, creating art that vanishes with the sun's warmth.
